name: Hasan al-Muthallath
title: Surviving Child
shortDescription: The son of Hasan al-Muthanna and Fatima bint Hussain who was present in the captives' caravan as a young child (or in his mother's womb) and suffered captivity.
fullStory:
Hasan ibn Hasan ibn Hasan (known as Hasan al-Muthallath) is the grandson of Imam Hasan Mujtaba (AS). His father was Hasan al-Muthanna (who fought and was wounded on Ashura) and his mother was Fatima bint Hussain (Imam Hussain's daughter).

According to some historical accounts, Fatima bint Hussain was pregnant during the event of Karbala and Hasan al-Muthallath was born after Ashura, while in other accounts he was a young child in the caravan.

In any case, he was considered the youngest survivor of Imam Hasan's (AS) lineage in Karbala, who along with his mourning mother and wounded father, witnessed (or endured in the womb) the suffering of captivity and the insults of Yazid's armies on the route to Kufa and Sham.

He later grew up in Medina and, during the Abbasid caliphate, due to his opposition to Mansur al-Dawaniqi's oppression, was arrested and martyred in a dark prison in Kufa. Like his ancestors, he sacrificed his life fighting against oppression.
quoteArabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنّا نَشْكُو إِلَيْكَ ظُلْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ...
quote: (Condition of his family:) O God! We complain to you of the oppressors' cruelty, that they were captured in childhood and killed in prison in youth.
